actions.detail: high impact ai | high impact decision

policies.name: cms_ai_policy_human_review
policies.description: Use of AI tools to draft CMS policies is followed by human review; AI supports but does not substitute for human decisions.

policies.name: cms_ai_provenance_verified
policies.description: The provenance and integrity of AI components (models, data, code) used in production are verified.

policies.name: cms_continuous_human_oversight
policies.description: Continuous human oversight is in place to ensure AI/ML output meets CMS and federal guidelines.

policies.name: cms_data_residency
policies.description: Foreign-entity AI tools are used only if deployed on CMS infrastructure with no data sent to the internet (CMS data stays in the U.S.).

policies.name: cms_external_ai_data_agreement
policies.description: External AI tools that can access non-public CMS data have appropriate data-use agreements in place beforehand.

policies.name: cms_high_impact_human_final_decision
policies.description: For high-impact cases, AI provides advice/recommendations only; the final decision is made by qualified staff with documented oversight.

policies.name: cms_high_impact_risk_assessed
policies.description: High-impact AI use cases (per OMB M-25-21) undergo a CMS AI governance risk assessment and apply minimum risk-management practices.

policies.name: cms_privacy_preserving_nonprod
policies.description: Development and lower environments use synthetic or de-identified data rather than real PII/PHI.

policies.name: cms_production_observability
policies.description: Production AI systems implement observability — traces, evaluations (EVALs), and key metrics tracked over time.

policies.name: cms_prompts_versioned
policies.description: AI system prompts used in production are versioned and their performance reviewed over time.

policies.name: cms_records_retention
policies.description: AI-supported official actions and AI-generated content are retained per records-retention and FOIA requirements.

policies.name: cms_sensitive_data_compliant_tool
policies.description: PHI / sensitive PII and other sensitive data are used only with AI tools that meet HHS/CMS cybersecurity & privacy standards.

policies.name: cms_zero_trust_for_ai
policies.description: AI tools that can make outbound requests are constrained by data minimization and network segmentation to contain the blast radius.
